channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Clashes in Syria's coastal region between government forces and Assad loyalists kill more than 70
item.description: LATAKIA, Syria (AP) — Clashes between Syrian security forces under the country's new, Islamist authorities and gunmen loyal to ousted President Bashar Assad in Syria's western coastal region have killed more than 70 people and left an area outside government control, a war monitor said Friday.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former, active-duty Army soldiers charged in scheme allegedly selling sensitive military information to China
item.description: Three U.S. Army soldiers – two active-duty and one former – have been indicted for their alleged roles in gathering and selling sensitive information to China, the DOJ said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: A San Carlos Apache teen who was reported missing and then found dead marks one of the latest tragedies as Native American communities continue to grapple with the epidemic of people being killed or going missing at high rates.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Adnan Syed to stay free after judge decides on time served for his murder sentence in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Adnan Syed, whose case amassed a worldwide following of “Serial” podcast listeners, will remain free — even though his murder conviction still stands, a Baltimore judge ruled on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gavin Newsom splits with Dems on trans athletes, prompting backlash from LGBTQ+ advocates
item.description: In a rebuke against the Democratic Party, California Gov. Gavin Newsom has revealed his thoughts on transgender athletes competing in girls' sporting events, and it has spurred reactions from across the state.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Mexico adult dies with measles, though cause of death isn't yet confirmed
item.description: An adult who was infected with measles has died in New Mexico, state health officials announced Thursday, though the virus has not been confirmed as the cause. The person who died was unvaccinated and did not seek medical care, a state health department spokesperson said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: Officials in the Western U.S. who protect the public from avalanches are sounding the alarm about the Trump administration firing hundreds of meteorologists and other scientists.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, KRON4

item.title: Hunter Biden asks for laptop data case to be dismissed, citing financial problems
item.description: “Plaintiff has suffered a significant downturn in his income and has significant debt in the millions of dollars range,” Hunter Biden’s attorneys wrote in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Princeton preppy murder suspect’s parents are no-shows for accused killer son's court appearance
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his brother and a family cat allegedly told police he had a "fit of madness" when they responded to the scene of the crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DNC taps far-left progressive to revitalize party and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger's defense claims he has autism in bid to avoid firing squad
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's autism diagnosis should take the death penalty off the table if he is convicted of the Idaho student murders, defense lawyer argues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants clone Border Patrol vehicles in bid to bypass Trump's crackdown
item.description: Some human smugglers have turned to cloning Border Patrol vehicles to bypass President Trump's crackdown on illegal border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A youth theater production rises from the ashes of the Los Angeles fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The day after her house burned down, Lara Ganz sent a group message to the youth theater troupe she runs: They would not let the Los Angeles firestorm stop their upcoming show.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House hunting surges where LA wildfires raged: Report
item.description: As Los Angeles residents recover from wildfires, house-hunting in many of the communities affected by the fires is reportedly surging.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Avalanche buries 3 skiers near Anchorage, Alaska, officials say
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers who had flown by helicopter to a remote mountain range were swept away by an avalanche and likely killed under the weight of dozens of feet of snow, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday. If the deaths are confirmed, it would be the deadliest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans hammer mayors of Boston, Chicago, Denver and New York over 'sanctuary city' policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican members of Congress hammered four Democratic mayors Wednesday about their so-called sanctuary city policies, accusing them of endangering Americans and threatening to prosecute local officials.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Things to know about avalanches, what causes them and how to stay safe
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers are believed dead after an avalanche swept them away and buried them under a pile of snow nearly 10 stories deep in the Alaska backcountry. Each winter about 25 people on average die in avalanches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Two charged in Kansas City Chiefs fans' deaths 14 months after bodies found frozen in backyard
item.description: Two men have been arrested in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found frozen in the backyard of a Kansas City, Missouri, area home back in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles County sues Southern California Edison, alleging utility's equipment sparked wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County said Wednesday that it's suing Southern California Edison, alleging the utility’s equipment sparked January’s Eaton Fire, which destroyed more than 9,400 structures and killed 17 people in the Altadena area.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two Detroit children in a van died from carbon monoxide not excessive cold, autopsies show
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two Detroit children who recently died in a van in below-freezing temperatures were victims of carbon monoxide, not exposure to cold weather, authorities said Wednesday. Police had said that Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's address to Congress
item.description: President Trump delivered the first congressional address of his second term on Tuesday evening. It was a chance for the president to underscore the sweeping changes he has wrought in the six weeks since he returned to the White House — and to defend those moves from his many critics.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Florida attorney general's office is investigating Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida's attorney general announced Tuesday that his office has opened a criminal investigation into Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, days after they returned to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Johnson retires from Fox Sports after being face of NFL coverage for most of 3 decades
item.description: Jimmy Johnson, the Pro Football Hall of Fame coach who won two Super Bowls and a college national championship, announced his retirement from Fox Sports on Monday after being part of its NFL coverage for most of the network's 31 years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hateful message left on Ohio restaurant receipt results in outpouring of support, staff says
item.description: The public is rallying behind a central Ohio Mexican restaurant after a customer left a hateful message instead of a tip.
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GOP bill to ban trans athletes from women’s sports blocked in Senate
item.description: Legislation to prevent transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports failed to advance in the Senate on Monday after all Democrats voted against it. The Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act failed to clear an initial procedural hurdle on a 51-45 vote.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9
